Impact domain: Aggregate user value of IOOS RA data
IOOS component: IOOS Regional Association (RA) websites - 9 of 11 RAs surveyed
User group: Combined employee (organizational) and individual data users of RA websites
Decision supported: General investment justification / appropriations support for IOOS
Economic pathway: Stated willingness-to-pay (contingent valuation) for free public-good data
Metric: Total estimated annual user value: $198.0 million (point estimate); 95% margin-of-error range $192.45-$207.52 million
Source: Colgan, Charles S. and Castelletto, Anthony. 2021/2023. The U.S. Integrated Ocean Observing System (IOOS): A Prototype User Valuation. Center for the Blue Economy, MIIS.
Limitations: This is a stated willingness-to-pay / user-surplus estimate from a self-selected (opt-in) survey, not a measured avoided cost or realized economic benefit; 2 of 11 RAs (NANOOS, MARACOOS) did not participate; users who access data only via direct server connections (e.g., NWS, Coast Guard) were excluded; authors describe the figure as likely an underestimate; this is explicitly labeled a 'prototype' study.
Claim allowed: COL could cautiously state that a 2021/2023 Center for the Blue Economy prototype study estimated the annual willingness-to-pay value of data from 9 of the 11 IOOS Regional Associations at approximately $198 million (range ~$192-208 million), based on a contingent-valuation survey of website users, while noting this is a conservative, prototype-stage estimate that excludes two RAs and non-website data users.
Update frequency: Static; study authors recommend replication approximately every 5 years or after major changes in data scope/quality
AI extraction notes: Extracted directly from full PDF mirror hosted at ioosassociation.org, since cbe.miis.edu blocked direct PDF fetch via bot detection. National total reconciles Table 16/17/19 figures from the report. Original extraction characterized evidence as moderate-high based on survey margin of error and sample size; mapped to Modeled because the estimate is survey-based contingent valuation. IOOS attribution mapped to Strong because the valued data services are IOOS Regional Association data services.
